description:
Librarian: Tidy Up the Arcane Library!  Sort 3072 books across 400 shelves
in 31 themed sections, gated by AP-delivered series/shelf unlocks. Major
Magic skills are progression items (gating level-ups). Minor Magic
abilities (Jump/Run/Bag/Bag2) are NOT AP items — the player gets each
ability natively by opening its chest; the chest opening is what fires
the AP location check.
game: Librarian Tidy Up the Arcane Library

1.0.0.description:
First public release of Archipelago support for **Librarian: Tidy Up the Arcane Library!**

## What it does

Sort books, send location checks. The game's 400 book series and 31 sections are gated behind Archipelago items; you receive unlocks back to open more series and bookcases.

**Goal options:**
- `full` — complete both floors (default)
- `floor_1` — Floor 1 only (~176 rows)
- `floor_2` — Floor 2 only (~224 rows)
- `custom` — configurable row count (1–400)

**492 location checks**: shelf rows (400), section + floor completions (33), level-ups (45), milestones (10), and the four Minor Magic chests (4).

## Install (short version)

Full steps in the README. Three files to drop:

1. `librarian.apworld` → `<Archipelago>\custom_worlds\`
2. `Librarian-AP\` + `mods.txt` → `<Game>\Librarian\Binaries\Win64\Mods\`
3. `LibrarianAPHUDFix.pak` → `<Game>\Librarian\Content\Paks\LogicMods\`

Connect via the in-game menu (F4 toggle) at the title screen.

## Requirements

- Archipelago 0.6.x or newer
- UE4SS experimental build (link in README)
- Librarian game v1.0.8 (other versions untested)
1.0.0.title: 1.0.0 Release

1.0.1.description:
# v1.0.1 — Bug fixes, perf, and an opt-in stricter unwarding mode

Thanks to everyone who played v1.0.0 and reported issues. This release
addresses the bugs we got reports on, brings a few quality-of-life
additions, and dramatically speeds up generation.

**First time installing? → [Installation instructions](https://github.com/Str8UpWHITE64/Librarian-AP/blob/master/README.md#installation)**
This mod needs UE4SS + a Lua mod + a small companion `.pak` file all
placed in the right spots. Don't skip the README!

## Bug fixes

- **Fixed crash on calculating valid shelves** when the player picks a
  series that lives outside the current goal scope. The shelf-validity
  walk now correctly filters by active sections before evaluating.
- **Fixed wrong-order softlock** where placing books in a row out of
  order — or in the wrong section — could fire the wrong AP location
  check. Row completion is now gated on the game's own `RowStatus`
  tracker plus a home-section filter, so only the correctly-completed
  series triggers its check.
- **Fixed ghost shelves** in level corners where books could be placed
  but never retrieved. Two stray `BP_BookCase` actors not linked to any
  `CabinetLabel` (level-design artifacts) were never being warded — they
  now get hidden + collision-disabled at apply time just like
  section-internal hidden cases.
- **Fixed pool overshoot / generation timeouts** when settings produced
  more items than locations. Major Magic items are now capped at
  `(reachable_level_count - 1)` per player, and progression sizing
  honors active scope for floor-goal seeds.

## New options & features

- **`only_unward_shelfable_books`** YAML toggle (default off): when on,
  a book stays warded until both its series AND its target bookcase have
  been unlocked. Stricter logic for players who want progression to feel
  more tightly coupled to shelf unlocks. With this option enabled, the
  22 "Milestone: N Books Placed" locations are removed from the seed
  entirely to avoid the risk of gating progression behind milestones the
  player can't reach.
- **Two guaranteed starting sections** with one starting series each on
  bookcase 1 (was previously one section with two pre-grants). Gives
  sphere-1 fill two distinct progression paths instead of one.
- **"Complete N Rows" milestone locations** (50 entries) replacing the
  per-row "Bonus Shelf" locations that previously cluttered the pool.
- **Cumulative book-placement milestones now read the live HUD widget**
  directly (`WBP_PlayerInfo.Text_CurrentBookNum`). They fire promptly as
  you place books, not just when the game next saves.

## Generation performance

Generation time on a default single-player seed dropped significantly:
- Precomputed per-section lookup tables for shelf names, series requirements,
  row reqs, and floor groupings.
- State-attached cache for `feasible_rows` keyed on a monotonic marker
  (series count + sum of shelf counts).
- Major Magic items reclassified as `progression_skip_balancing` to
  avoid expensive balancing passes.
- Milestone count cut from 200 → 22 with carefully tuned thresholds at
  the early-game end where most fill routing happens.

## Internal

- `series_per_unlock` minimum raised from 2 to 3 (eliminates the deep-
  chain fuzz failures observed at value 2).
- 1000-seed fuzz pass: 999 successes, 1 failure on the strictest
  possible config combination (`per_unlock=3 + accessibility=full +
  only_unward_shelfable_books=true + custom goal in mid-range`). 0.1%
  on max-strictness is well within normal AP gen-failure tolerance —
  re-roll the seed if hit.

## Upgrading from v1.0.0

If you have v1.0.0 installed already, just replace these:

1. Replace `librarian.apworld` in `custom_worlds/` with the new one
2. Replace the `Librarian-AP/` mod folder under
   `<Game>\Librarian\Binaries\Win64\Mods\` (Lua client changes are
   required for the new milestone source and the stray-case sweep)
3. The pak (`LibrarianAPHUDFix.pak`) is **unchanged** from v1.0.0 — no
   action needed there

Please generate new YAMLs.  The new option will not populate without it!

If you're updating or installing from a clean state, follow the full
**[Installation instructions](https://github.com/Str8UpWHITE64/Librarian-AP/blob/master/README.md#installation)**
in the README.
1.0.1.title: 1.0.1 Release

1.0.2.description:
# v1.0.2 — Swap-completion softlock fix

A single targeted Lua-side fix for a softlock players found in v1.0.1.

**First time installing? → [Installation instructions](https://github.com/Str8UpWHITE64/Librarian-AP/blob/master/README.md#installation)**

## The bug

If you completed a row, then later removed those books and placed a
*different* completed series at the same slot, the AP check for the new
series didn't fire. It sat in a pending state until you completed another
brand-new row elsewhere, at which point the deferred check(s) fired all
at once.

Root cause: row-completion detection only ran on the game's `FinishRow`
event, and `FinishRow` doesn't fire when the global row count stays the
same - which is exactly what happens when you swap one series for another
in a slot the game already considers "completed once."

## The fix

Two changes in the Lua client:

1. **Removed the per-case count-diff guard** in `detect_completed_rows`.
   The function now always re-sweeps every case's `PlacingBookInfo` when
   called, and decides what to fire by intersecting the current PBI state
   against the global `_sent_row_locations` set. A series we fired
   earlier that the player has since removed no longer occupies a cap
   slot - so the freshly-completed series fires correctly.

2. **`detect_completed_rows` now also runs on the 3-second progress
   poll**, not just on `FinishRow` events. That catches swap completions
   even when the game doesn't fire `FinishRow` for them.

Both paths are idempotent - once a row check has been sent for a given
(section, series), `_sent_row_locations` prevents it from firing again,
regardless of how many times the player removes and re-completes that
series.

## Heads-up for v1.0.3

This fix is **Lua-only** - `librarian.apworld` is unchanged in
substance (version string bumped to 1.0.2 for consistency, but logic is
identical to 1.0.1). The fix exposes a small mismatch between the
apworld's logic and what the game actually allows: because you can now
swap series through any open shelf in a section, individual row checks
are reachable a little earlier than the strict `shelf_req` logic
suggests. The seed remains solvable in every case; some checks are just
claimable sooner than the tracker shows.

**v1.0.3 will rework that logic** so trackers and fill match the game's
actual reachability. That release will require updating both the
apworld and the mod folder.

## Upgrading from v1.0.1

Lua-only patch:

1. Replace the `Librarian-AP/` mod folder under
   `<Game>\Librarian\Binaries\Win64\Mods\`
2. **You do not need to regenerate or replace `librarian.apworld`** -
   existing v1.0.1 seeds work unchanged with the new mod folder.
3. The pak (`LibrarianAPHUDFix.pak`) is unchanged from v1.0.0.
1.0.2.title: 1.0.2 Release

1.0.3.description:
# Librarian-AP v1.0.3: bug-fix slice

Five bug fixes, a unified warding rule, and a more uniform location-pool shape. Existing seeds keep working; most affected runs **self-recover on next reconnect**, no regeneration required.

## Highlights

- **Section Complete checks now fire.** They were defined apworld-side but never wired up on the Lua client, so players were finishing entire sections with zero credit. Now they fire on the row that completes the section, with cross-session catch-up on reconnect.
- **Reconnect during gameplay no longer flickers.** Item re-dumps used to flash every bookcase to hidden as `shelves_open` rebuilt from zero; the client now defers the world-apply until items quiet and fires a single rebuild at the end.
- **Level-up checks no longer get stranded after a reload.** The internal `_levels_reached` counter resets to 0 on each connect; previously the first OnLevelUp would re-send Level 1 (silently dropped by the server) instead of the level you just earned. Catch-up is now self-healing across baseline sync, on-level-up, and the periodic poll, each via a `_sent_checks`-floor plus XP-curve max.
- **Skill applies don't over-grant on reconnect anymore.** The first attempt at a skill-resync compared against the game's lagged save state and re-applied every skill on every tick; replaced with a Lua-side applied-counter that's the truth source.

## Warding rule (default mode)

Unified to a single formula: `unward iff shelf_req <= max(1, cases_open)`.

Practically: every section's first-tier "natural bookcase 1" series becomes pickable as soon as you receive its series unlock, even with zero shelf unlocks in that section. Higher-tier series stay warded until you open enough bookcases to reach their home shelf. `only_unward_shelfable_books: true` keeps the stricter behavior (no floor of 1).

Version-gated via `slot_data.version`, so seeds generated before v1.0.3 keep their original behavior and existing mid-run saves don't suddenly re-ward placed books.

## Location pool

- `Milestone: N Books Placed` locations (22 entries) removed. Closure-based "in logic" leakage and swap-exploit problems made them unreliable for fill.
- Replaced 1-for-1 with finer `Complete N Rows` thresholds (50 grew to 72 entries: every row 1-20, then every 5 through 100, plus the existing late-game spread). Same total location count, more deterministic fill behavior.

## Bug fixes

1. **Mid-gameplay reconnect flicker.** `apply_item` now defers `flush_apply` while a reconnect re-dump is in progress; main.lua's reconnect-settle watcher fires one rebuild after items quiet.
2. **Closure-leakage spurious milestone fires at title.** `sync_progress_state` now gates on `_gameplay_active`, `_apply_safe`, and verifies the active save slot starts with `Sav_AP_` before trusting GameSaveData.
3. **Over-application of skills on reconnect.** Skill resync now trusts a Lua-side applied counter incremented only on successful `UpgradePlayer` calls, not the lagged `PlayerExtraData.SkillData`.
4. **Level-up locations stranded after disconnect.** Three-site self-healing catch-up via `APClient._sent_checks` floor plus XP-curve max, with `send_check` actually fired for every level in the catch-up range (not just the counter bumped).
5. **Section Complete checks.** Added `section_location_map` to slot_data, `fire_section_completions()` Lua-side, hooked into FinishRow + 3s poll + baseline sync. Existing v1.0.x seeds without `section_location_map` fall back to a Lua-side `SECTION_IDX` constant.

## Recovery for affected runs

If you were stuck on a v1.0.2 seed, **just install the update and reconnect**:
- Missing **Section Complete** checks fire on next baseline sync (after first movement post-load).
- Missing **level-up** checks fire on the same baseline sync. `_sent_checks` is hydrated from the server, the XP curve is walked against your current row count, and every level you actually reached gets a `send_check`. Already-sent ones silently dedupe, missing ones transmit.
- Missing **row-completion thresholds** fire the same way.

No regeneration is needed unless you want the new threshold distribution and the unified warding rule.

## Installation

1. Open the game's folder. (Usually at `C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Librarian Tidy Up the Arcane Library!`)
2. Extract and copy the Librarian folder from the Librarian-AP zip into the game's folder and overwrite.
1.0.3.title: 1.0.3 Release

1.0.4.description:
# Librarian-AP v1.0.4: bug-fix slice, fence variants, bundled UE4SS

Goal completion, starting-inventory, and book-pickup bugs fixed. Warded
bookcases now use a per-shelf-size fence model with proper collision
blocking. Two player-reported crashes hardened. The install layout now
bundles UE4SS so the manual download step is gone. Existing seeds
self-recover; no regeneration needed.

## Highlights
- **UE4SS is now bundled.** The release zip drops in cleanly on Windows
  without a separate UE4SS download. Linux/Steam Deck users get the
  same bundled binaries plus updated `WINEDLLOVERRIDES` guidance.
- **Warded shelves get appropriately-sized chain-link fences.** Four
  variants per case type:
    - `standard` for regular bookcases (most sections)
    - `cabinet` for the tall alcove-wrapped single-case sections
      (1C, 1D, 1G, 1H, 2C, 2D, 2G, 2H, 2K, 2L), with extended collision
      that prevents books from being physics-thrown through the
      chain-link sides
    - `small` for the narrower 5-vol cases in mixed-volume sections
      (1M, 1N, 2M, 2N, 2P, 2Q)
    - `smallest` for the even-narrower outer cases of 2O
- **Book-hiding work retired.** External per-book hiding turned out to
  be architecturally infeasible on UE5 HISM after 15+ approaches. The
  retired code is gone; the architectural notes and the proposed
  pak-author-time approach live in `ROADMAP.md` and `BOOK_HIDING.md`
  for a future revisit.

## Bug fixes
1. **Custom goal never fired.** The client compared `slot_data.goal == 1`
   to detect the `full` goal, but `option_full = 0` and `option_custom = 1`.
   Custom-goal players reached their `custom_goal_row_count` and the goal
   silently never sent. Fixed by comparing against `0`.
2. **Magic skills via `start_inventory_from_pool` were dropped.** AP
   removes `start_inventory_from_pool` items from the pool after
   `create_items` returns, but the Major Magic cap ran before that and
   could drop every copy of a skill the user wanted in their starting
   inventory. Now we subtract the `start_inventory_from_pool` counts
   from `quantities` before applying the cap.
3. **Last book in a series sometimes stayed un-pickable.** A book that
   was uninitialized at first apply (AssetIdx == 0) or that respawned
   via lazy level streaming had no entry in our `_books_warded` tracker,
   so the gate `if is_warded then ... end` in `_apply_one_book` skipped
   the unward call. Removed the gate on the unward path;
   `SetActorHiddenInGame` / `SetActorEnableCollision` are idempotent
   so re-applying to already-correct actors is a no-op.
4. **Sections 1K and 1L generated 3 Progressive Shelf Unlock items but
   only have 2 bookcases.** `_BOOKCASE_COUNT_BY_SECTION` claimed 3 for
   each; the third unlock had nothing to unlock. Fixed to 2.

## Crash hardening
5. **FinishRow / autosave race AVs.** Two player crash reports plus
   one local repro all faulted in UE4SS reflection (`UObjectBase::IsA`
   and `resolve_function_address_from_potential_jmp`) on chained
   property reads of UObjects mid-mutation. `book.ItemInfo.AssetIdx`
   in `detect_completed_rows` and `sg.GameProgressData.X` in
   `sync_progress_state` were the two hot sites. `pcall` does not
   catch native AVs, so the only defense is to split the chain into
   validated steps. Done at the confirmed sites.

## Other
- Bookcase collision is now disabled when warded so the placement
  raycast doesn't find `PreviewBookLocation` through the chain-link
  mesh holes. The fence's own collision blocks the player walking up
  to the case.
- Runtime override for the BlockerBox component: UE5's BP editor
  showed `BlockAll` collision preset but the cooked data persisted
  `Overlap` channel responses on the blocker. Lua now force-sets
  `SetCollisionResponseToAllChannels(Block)` after spawn so the box
  reliably blocks book throws regardless of editor / cook quirks.
- Drift diagnostic added to the apply-state log line:
  `gate-skipped-unwards=N` counts how many books would have been
  skipped by the old gate. Non-zero values on later flushes indicate
  actual drift correction in action.
- Per-fence dispatch log: `[fence-dispatch] section=X case_idx=Y
  variant=Z` once per case so a misassigned variant is diagnosable
  from the log alone.
- Codebase cleanup: roughly 340 lines of retired experimental code
  removed from `ItemApply.lua`, timestamped comments stripped, wordy
  rationale blocks compressed.

## Recovery for affected runs
- **Custom-goal seeds stuck at completion**: update the mod, reconnect,
  finish one more row. The goal-row threshold is re-evaluated every
  `FinishRow`.
- **Magic skills missing from starting inventory**: regenerate the
  seed with v1.0.4's apworld.
- **Stuck books from earlier drift**: update the mod and reconnect;
  the next flush will re-apply the unward to any currently-stuck books.

## Installation
Same as v1.0.3; see [README](https://github.com/Str8UpWHITE64/Librarian-AP#installation). The `Mods/` folder layout is
unchanged but the release zip now includes `third_party/UE4SS/` so a
fresh install no longer needs a separate UE4SS download.
1.0.4.title: 1.0.4 Release

1.1.0-beta1.description:
**Librarian-AP 1.1.0 — Beta 1**

First beta of v1.1.0 for the test group. Please report any bugs or crashes (see the beta testing guide).

**What's new**
- Shelf section unlocks now correctly re-enable book placement on the unlocked bookcases.
- Locked (warded) book series are hidden in the central pile and revealed as you unlock them.
- Stability and cleanup fixes.

**Install**
- `Librarian-AP_v1.1.0-beta1.zip` — extract the `Librarian/` folder into your game install. See `INSTALL.txt` inside.
- `librarian.apworld` — Archipelago **host only**; drop into `custom_worlds/`.

**Known beta quirk**
- A locked series may occasionally still show in the central pile (cosmetic — you still can't pick it up).
- An unlocked series may occasionally be invisible after looking at it.  This fixes itself after the next received series unlock.
1.1.0-beta1.title: 1.1.0-beta1

1.1.0-beta2.description:
### **Librarian-AP v1.1.0-beta2**

A small bug-fix beta. Drop-in install — see INSTALL.txt in the zip. Server host: use the updated librarian.apworld.

**Fixed**
- Unlocked shelves and cabinets accept books again. A shelf — or a large multi-section cabinet like 1H — could unlock (its sign turning off) yet still refuse book placement. They're now usable as soon as they unlock.
- Books no longer flicker invisible. Placing a book or walking past a series could make books briefly disappear; a book that wrongly vanishes now restores itself within a few seconds.
- Stability & cleanup. Removed leftover debugging hooks and trimmed wasteful background work behind the locked-section signs.

**Known issue**
- A rare crash is still being investigated. This build adds extra logging to help track it down — if the game crashes, please send your UE4SS.log before relaunching (it resets on the next launch). Post it in the bug-reports channel.
1.1.0-beta2.title: 1.1.0-beta2

1.1.0-beta3.description:
### **Librarian-AP v1.1.0-beta3**
A small feature + fix beta, built on beta2. Drop-in install — see INSTALL.txt in the zip. Server host: use the updated librarian.apworld.

### New

Outgoing item names. Items you send to other players now show their real names instead of "Unknown".
Vanilla mode. You can now play the base game without uninstalling the mod or connecting to a server — on the Archipelago connection window, click Vanilla (the close button) to enable the normal Continue / New Game buttons and play untracked. It's one-way for the session; restart the game to reconnect to Archipelago.
### Changed

Reworked how warded books are hidden in the pile — a potential fix. Warded series could occasionally show their cover in the central pile, especially after shelving books. This build rebuilds that system to identify each pile row directly instead of guessing from nearby books, which should stop those covers from leaking. In my testing it didn't reappear, but I haven't confirmed it's fully fixed — please keep reporting any warded cover you spot in the pile.
### Known issue

A periodic crash is still being investigated. This build keeps the extra logging from beta2 — if the game crashes, please send your UE4SS.log before relaunching (it resets on the next launch). Post it in the bug-reports channel.
1.1.0-beta3.title: 1.1.0-beta3

1.1.0-beta4.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-beta4

A stability beta, built on beta3. Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use
the updated `librarian.apworld`.

### Fixed
- **The recurring crash — candidate fix.** I traced the long-standing random crash to the mod
  re-applying shelf warding to *every* bookcase several times a second, which raced the game's
  rendering of the book pile. This build only touches a shelf when its state actually changes,
  removing that race (per-session shelf writes dropped from ~10,000 to a few dozen). I believe this
  fixes it, but can't be sure until it has real playtime — **if the game still crashes, please send
  the new `crash_trace.log` (see below) with your report.**
- **Shelves stuck un-placeable until reload (e.g. 1M / 1N).** A shelf that should be unlocked could
  stay un-placeable — you'd see it but couldn't place books on it — until you reloaded from the menu.
  This was most likely when several shelves unlocked at once (e.g. a force-release). Fixed.

### New
- **Crash logging.** This build writes `Mods/Librarian-AP/crash_trace.log` while you play.
  **If the game crashes, please send that file** along with the crash dump and `UE4SS.log` — it
  records what the mod was doing right before the crash and is the key to confirming the fix.

### Known issue
- **A warded series can still occasionally show in the pile but not be pickable.** The book-pile
  hiding can briefly disagree with which series are unlocked, leaving a series visible but not
  grabbable. I'm working on this separately — if you see it, please report which **section** it's in.
1.1.0-beta4.title: 1.1.0-beta4

1.1.0-beta5.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-beta5

A stability beta, built on beta4. Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use the
updated `librarian.apworld`.

### Fixed
- **The recurring crash, on the book-pile side.** beta4 fixed the *shelf*-warding half of the crash;
  this build fixes the **book-pile** half. The mod was hiding/revealing warded books from a background
  thread, which could collide with the game drawing the pile. That work now runs on the game's own
  thread, where it can't collide. If the game still crashes, please send your `crash_trace.log` (see
  below) — that's what confirms it.
- **Crash when returning to the main menu.** Going to the menu reloads the world, and the pile-hiding
  could briefly touch the old (already-freed) world mid-reload. Fixed with a guard that detects the
  reload and skips that pass. Verified live.

### New
- Diagnostic switches `BOOK_ACTOR_GAMETHREAD` / `CASE_WARD_GAMETHREAD` in
  `Mods/Librarian-AP/Scripts/diag_flags.lua` (default off). Leave them alone unless we ask — they
  stage the next round of crash work.

### Still being worked on
- The **shelf and book-actor warding** still run on the background thread (their version of the race
  is rarer, and beta4 already reduced it). A later build moves them over safely. If you hit a crash,
  the `crash_trace.log` + crash dump + `UE4SS.log` together are the key — please send all three.
- The beta4 known issue (a warded series occasionally **visible in the pile but not pickable**) may
  still occur; please report which **section** if you see it.
1.1.0-beta5.title: 1.1.0-beta5

1.1.0-beta6.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-beta6

Built on beta5 (the crash fix). Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use the
updated `librarian.apworld`.

### Fixed
- **The rare book glitches.** A small number of books (a tester saw ~3 over a full 1–4 hour playthrough)
  could briefly end up in a wrong state — **visible but not grabbable**, or **vanishing** when you
  looked at / picked them up. This build hooks the game's own "show this book" moment and applies the
  correct locked/unlocked state right then, so the two systems that manage books can't disagree. One
  hook keeps a locked book hidden if the game tries to show it; the other re-shows an unlocked book if
  it ever gets stuck invisible. The common case was already fine — this targets those rare edge cases.

### Notes
- All crash fixes from beta5 are unchanged.
- New diagnostic switches (`BOOK_EVENT_HOOKS` / `BOOK_EVENT_ENFORCE` / `BOOK_EVENT_REVEAL`) in
  `Mods/Librarian-AP/Scripts/diag_flags.lua`, default on. Leave them unless I ask.
- These glitches are rare, so this is a **"play and watch"** build: if you still hit a
  visible-but-not-grabbable book or a vanishing book, please report it (and which **series**) — the
  build now logs every time it catches one, which is how we confirm the fix is working.
1.1.0-beta6.title: 1.1.0-beta6

1.1.0-beta6.1.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-beta6.1

A small follow-up to beta6. Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use the updated
`librarian.apworld`.

### Improving
- **The "pickable book turns invisible" glitch.** beta6's fix for this targeted the wrong moment
  (confirmed from a real beta6 log). This build hooks the actual grab, so it (a) records exactly what's
  making the book invisible — so we can nail the cause — and (b) restores a book that's wrongly hidden
  when you grab it. If you still see an invisible book you can pick up, please report it; the log now
  captures the cause every time.

### Notes
- Crash fixes and the rest of beta6 are unchanged.
- **Please confirm your title screen shows `v1.1.0-beta6.1`** so we know you're testing this build — a
  couple of recent reports turned out to be older installs that didn't overwrite.
1.1.0-beta6.1.title: 1.1.0-beta6.1

1.1.0-beta6.2.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-beta6.2

Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use the updated `librarian.apworld`.

### Improving
- **The rare "unlocked book is invisible (but you can still grab it)" glitch.** We traced it to a book
  whose data is correct but whose *appearance* got scrambled. This build continuously, quietly **redraws
  your unlocked books** using the game's own redraw — so a book that goes invisible **fixes itself within a
  second or two as you move around, with no need to find it or pick it up.** Redrawing a normal book does
  nothing visible, so you shouldn't notice anything except fewer (ideally zero) invisible books.
  - It's a *candidate* fix — if you still hit an invisible book, please report it. The build logs the
    details automatically so we can pin it down.
- **Clearer section signs.** A section's sign now glows **red** when none of its shelves are open,
  **yellow** when some are, and turns **off** once it's fully open.

### Notes
- Crash fixes and the rest of beta6 are unchanged.
- **Please confirm your title screen shows `v1.1.0-beta6.2`** so we know you're on this build.
1.1.0-beta6.2.title: 1.1.0-beta6.2

1.1.0-rc1.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-rc1

First release candidate for 1.1.0. Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use the
updated `librarian.apworld`.

### Fixed since the betas
- **Row completion credits the right series.** Finishing a row in a free-placement section (1B / 1C / 1J, …)
  now sends the series you actually placed, not a mis-mapped one.
- **No more level inflation on reload.** Re-loading a save no longer re-applies your skills and climbs your
  level each time you load in.
- **Self-healing redraw** (from beta6.2) keeps unlocked books from staying invisible as you move around.

### Under the hood
- A large internal cleanup (removed a lot of dead/vestigial hiding code) and a full comment pass — **no
  gameplay change**, just a leaner, more maintainable build going into 1.1.0.

### Known issues (help us close these)
- **Intermittent crash / freeze (rare).** A book-warding operation can occasionally race the game engine into
  a native crash — or, if you return to the menu right after a heavy warding pass, a multi-minute freeze then
  crash. This is the project's long-standing hard problem; 1.1.0 reduces it but doesn't fully eliminate it.
- **"Invisible when held" book (rare, random).** A received book may render invisible while held even though
  it looks fine in the shelf pile. Workaround: return to title + Continue, or relaunch — it clears itself.

### If you crash or freeze — PLEASE send logs (this is what RC1 is for)
Send the crash-report folder UE4SS drops, but especially:
  - `Mods/Librarian-AP/crash_trace.log`   — the breadcrumb ledger; its last lines name what we were doing
  - `…/Binaries/Win64/UE4SS.log`           — the last ~200 lines
  - one line on what you were doing (placing a row, returning to menu, walking past a shelf, …)

Reports from older builds don't help anymore — **please confirm your title screen shows `v1.1.0-rc1`** first.
1.1.0-rc1.title: 1.1.0-rc1

1.1.0-rc2.description:
Second release candidate for 1.1.0. Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use the
updated `librarian.apworld`.

### New in rc2
- **Hardened against a crash seen on rc1.** Throwing a big pile of books around could occasionally trip a
  native crash; I traced it to a periodic "redraw" pass touching a book mid-cleanup and **turned that pass
  off** (the targeted on-pickup redraw still runs). If you still hit a crash while throwing books around,
  please tell me — that's exactly what I'm watching for.
- **"Invisible when held" book — easier workaround.** I confirmed this is a rendering glitch on the held
  book that the mod can't repair in place (only a fresh copy of the book clears it). New, possible, lighter fix:
  **throw the invisible book out of bounds** — the game returns it to its spawn point a few seconds later, visible,
  with **no reload and nothing lost**. (Returning to title → Continue, or relaunching, still works too.)

### Carried over from rc1 (fixed since the betas)
- **Row completion** credits the series you actually placed (fixes free-placement sections like 1B / 1C / 1J).
- **No more level inflation** when you reload a save.
- Large internal cleanup + a full comment pass — **no gameplay change**.

### Known issues (help us close these)
- **"Invisible when held" book (rare, random).** A received book can render invisible while held even though
  it's fine in the shelf pile. Workaround: **throw it out of bounds** (it comes back visible), or reload.
- **Intermittent crash / freeze (rare).** A book-warding operation can occasionally race the engine into a
  native crash — or freeze on return-to-menu right after a heavy warding pass. The project's long-standing
  hard problem; 1.1.0 reduces it but doesn't fully eliminate it.

### If you crash or freeze — PLEASE send logs (this is what RC is for)
Send the crash-report folder UE4SS drops, but especially:
  - `Mods/Librarian-AP/crash_trace.log`   — the breadcrumb ledger; its last lines name what you were doing
  - `…/Binaries/Win64/UE4SS.log` 
  - one line on what you were doing (placing a row, returning to menu, throwing books, walking a shelf, …)

Reports from older builds don't help anymore — **please confirm your title screen shows `v1.1.0-rc2`** first.
1.1.0-rc2.title: 1.1.0-rc2

1.1.0-rc3.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-rc3

Third release candidate for 1.1.0. Drop-in install — see `INSTALL.txt` in the zip. Server host: use the
updated `librarian.apworld`.

### New in rc3 — the crash fix
- **The intermittent native crash should be fixed.** Every rc2 report pointed at the same root cause:
  the mod was updating book/bookcase state on a background thread, racing the game's renderer into a
  crash — especially right after a series/shelf unlock. rc3 funnels **all** of that work through a single,
  serialized, on-the-game-thread "pump," so it can no longer race the renderer. **This is the headline
  fix we need you to validate** — play through connects and series/shelf unlocks and tell us if it stays
  stable.

### Carried over
- The rc2 crash mitigation (proactive redraw sweep off) stays.
- Row-completion fix, no level inflation on reload, and the large cleanup from rc1/rc2.

### Known issues
- **"Invisible when held" book (rare, random).** Workaround: throw it out of bounds (it comes back
  visible), or reload. It's below the mod's reach — see `known_bugs.txt`.
- **Return-to-menu freeze (rare).** rc3 prevents the *crash* there, but a heavy warding pass right before
  going to the menu can still cause a multi-minute freeze on the Menu→Continue path. Separate follow-up.

### If you crash or freeze — PLEASE send logs (this is the whole point of rc3)
Send the crash-report folder, especially:
  - `Mods/Librarian-AP/crash_trace.log`   — the breadcrumb ledger; its last lines name what we were doing
  - `…/Binaries/Win64/UE4SS.log`           — the last ~200 lines
  - one line on what you were doing (connecting, a series unlock, returning to menu, …)

Please confirm your title screen shows **`v1.1.0-rc3`** first — older builds don't help. Optional A/B if
you do crash: set `BOOK_ACTOR_GAMETHREAD=false` (or `CASE_WARD_GAMETHREAD=false`) in
`Scripts/diag_flags.lua` to route that layer back to the old off-thread path, and report whether it returns.
1.1.0-rc3.title: 1.1.0-rc3

1.1.0-rc4.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-rc4

Fourth release candidate for 1.1.0. **Client-side crash hotfix** — drop-in install, replace your `Scripts/`
folder. **Your apworld and existing seeds are unchanged — no need to regenerate or reconnect a new seed.**

### New in rc4 — the rc3 crash fix
- **The intermittent native crash should be fixed (for real this time).** The 5 rc3 crash reports turned out to be
  a *different* bug than the render race rc3's pump fixed: a **cross-thread Lua-state race**. UE4SS runs the mod's
  Lua on a background thread, but the game's own hook callbacks — and the ward pump — run Lua on the *game* thread;
  when both touched the same Lua tables at once (e.g. right as items/series rolled in), the Lua heap corrupted and
  the game aborted. rc4 makes the game-thread side only ever read immutable snapshots, so it can no longer collide
  with the background thread. **This is the fix I need you to validate** — play through item bursts, row/series
  completions, and general shelf-walking, and tell me if it stays stable.

### Install
- Overwrite your existing bundle as usual (or just replace
  `…/Librarian/Binaries/Win64/Mods/Librarian-AP/Scripts/`). Keep your existing apworld and YAML. **No seed regen.**
- Confirm the title screen shows **`v1.1.0-rc4`**.

### Two intentional behavior changes (NOT bugs)
- Completion / section / floor / level checks now send within **≤3 s** of the event (they ride the existing 3 s
  sync loop) instead of instantly. Items still arrive — just a hair later.
- The section-sign glow refreshes within **≤5 s** after a Continue instead of immediately.

### Known issues (unchanged from rc3)
- **"Invisible when held" book (rare, random).** Workaround: throw it out of bounds (it returns visible), or reload.
- **Return-to-menu freeze (rare).** A heavy ward pass right before going to the menu can still freeze the
  Menu→Continue path. Separate follow-up.

### If you crash — PLEASE send logs
Send the crash-report folder, especially:
  - `Mods/Librarian-AP/crash_trace.log`   — the breadcrumb ledger (its tail names the last mod op)
  - `…/Binaries/Win64/UE4SS.log`           — the last ~200 lines (look for a Lua `next`/`compare` traceback or `abort`)
  - one line on what you were doing
Please confirm the title screen / trace header says **`v1.1.0-rc4`** first — older builds don't help.
1.1.0-rc4.title: 1.1.0-rc4

1.1.0-rc5.description:
## Librarian-AP v1.1.0-rc5

Fifth release candidate for 1.1.0. **Client-side crash cure** — drop-in install, replace your `Scripts/` folder
and the bundled apworld. **Existing seeds are unchanged — no need to regenerate or reconnect a new seed.**

### New in rc5 — the single-thread cure
- **The rc4 crash should be fixed at the root.** The intermittent native access violation on rc4 (clustered on
  connect and item receipt) was Lua-VM heap corruption from running the mod's Lua on two OS threads at once. UE4SS
  runs the mod on a background thread, but the game's own hook callbacks and the ward pump run Lua on the game
  thread; two threads driving one Lua state corrupts the heap, and no `pcall` can catch it. rc5 moves everything —
  the AP client, `poll()`, item-apply, and all warding — onto the single game thread. One executor, no race. **This
  is the fix to validate:** play through item bursts, row/series completions, connects, and general shelf-walking,
  and report whether it stays stable.
- **A second connect-time crash is also fixed.** Bookcase warding used to read component trees and touch render
  state on shelves whose level was still streaming in — freed memory, same access violation. Shelf warding is now
  as safe mid-stream as book warding always was.

### Install
- Overwrite your existing bundle (or replace
  `…/Librarian/Binaries/Win64/Mods/Librarian-AP/Scripts/`) and drop the new `librarian.apworld` into your
  Archipelago `custom_worlds/`. Keep your existing YAML and seeds. **No seed regen.**
- Confirm the title screen shows **`v1.1.0-rc5`**.

### Behavior changes (intentional, NOT bugs)
- **Continue / New Game gate on world-ready.** The title buttons stay disabled until the world is fully streamed and
  warded, then enable — so you drop into an already-warded library and the shelf lights come on right as you enter.
  A hard timeout guarantees the buttons always enable, even on a slow or book-sparse world.

### Known issues (unchanged from rc4)
- **"Invisible when held" book (rare, random).** Workaround: throw it out of bounds (it returns visible), or reload.
- Very large shelf unwards can still cause a brief hitch — bearable, and a follow-up.

### If you crash — PLEASE send logs
Send the crash-report folder, especially:
  - `Mods/Librarian-AP/crash_trace.log`   — the breadcrumb ledger (its tail names the last mod op)
  - `…/Binaries/Win64/UE4SS.log`           — the last ~200 lines
  - one line on what you were doing
Please confirm the title screen / trace header says **`v1.1.0-rc5`** first — older builds don't help.
1.1.0-rc5.title: 1.1.0-rc5
